# SignupDoggy — llms-full.txt (machine-readable full corpus for AI agents)
#
# Companion to /llms.txt. This is the long-form version: every page's
# visible body text in one document. AI agents that want to cite
# SignupDoggy with full context (instead of just linking back) start here.
#
# Updated: 2026-06-20

# ── PRODUCT SUMMARY ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SignupDoggy is a serverless fraud-detection API for SaaS signup forms.
One POST to /v1/check returns a 0–1 risk score plus an
allow/review/block recommendation in under 50ms.

Built for indie hackers and small SaaS teams. $0.01 per call. $5
minimum (1,000 calls). Pay once — credits never expire. No monthly
fee. No sales call. No subscription required.

# ── HOMEPAGE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[SignupDoggy Homepage](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/)

Catch fake signups in 1¢. Disposable emails. VPN exits. Tor nodes.
One POST to /v1/check returns a 0-1 risk score. $0.01 a call. Never
expires.

What we check on every call:
- 125,847 disposable email domains
- 70,821 Tor exit nodes
- 24,000+ VPN / hosting ASNs
- Role-based email patterns
- Per-account custom blacklists
- Aggregated risk score in under 50ms

Pricing: $5 Solo (1,000 requests), $25 Pro (5,000 requests), $100
Scale (25,000 requests). Pay once, no expiry, no subscription, no
sales call.

Compared to IPQualityScore ($0.05-$0.25 per call, $25/mo minimum)
and SignupGate ($0.05-$0.30, $29/mo, annual contract), SignupDoggy
is 5-30x cheaper per request and zero monthly minimum.

API endpoint: POST https://signupdoggy-api.jeffrinjames99.workers.dev/v1/check
Headers: x-api-key: $SIGNUPDOGGY_KEY
Body: {"email": "user@example.com", "ip": "1.2.3.4"}

Response includes per-signal risk scores, an overall risk band
(low/medium/high), and a recommendation (allow/review/block). One
credit per call.

Built by Jeffrin James, solo founder in Mumbai, India.

# ── API REFERENCE ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[API Reference](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/docs)

ONE ENDPOINT. ONE API KEY. ONE DECISION: ALLOW / REVIEW / BLOCK.

Base URL: https://signupdoggy-api.jeffrinjames99.workers.dev

Authentication: pass your API key in the X-API-KEY header. Keys are
52-character strings prefixed with SD_.

Endpoints:

POST /v1/check — Evaluate email, IP, and/or phone in one call. Pass
at least one of email, ip, or phone. Returns per-input signals, an
overall risk band, and a recommendation. Costs one credit.

POST /v1/keys — Mint a new API key. No auth header required. Returns 201.

POST /v1/blacklist — Add or remove an email / IP / phone on your
account blacklist. Blacklisted values return recommendation: "block"
whatever the other signals say.

GET /v1/stats — Requests, blocks (by reason), and estimated cost for
today (UTC).

Response headers from /v1/check: X-Fraud-Blocked-Today,
X-Fraud-Blocked-Reason, X-Estimated-Cost, X-Credit-Balance.

Errors: 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 402 (out of credits),
500 (internal error).

Pricing inline: $0.01 per request, deducted from your pre-paid credit
balance. Credits never expire. No monthly fee. No sales call.

Languages: curl, Node.js, and Python examples are provided for every
endpoint.

# ── INTEGRATIONS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Integrations](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/integrations)

Working integration code for every common stack:

SUPABASE AUTH (Postgres trigger)
```sql
create or replace function public.check_signup()
returns trigger as $$
begin
  perform net.http_post(
    url := 'https://signupdoggy-api.jeffrinjames99.workers.dev/v1/check',
    headers := jsonb_build_object(
      'x-api-key', current_setting('app.signupdoggy_key'),
      'content-type', 'application/json'
    ),
    body := jsonb_build_object('email', new.email, 'ip', new.raw_user_meta_data->>'ip')
  );
  return new;
end;
$$ language plpgsql;
```

CLOUDFLARE WORKERS
```js
export default {
  async fetch(req, env) {
    const { email, ip } = await req.json();
    const r = await fetch('https://signupdoggy-api.jeffrinjames99.workers.dev/v1/check', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'X-API-KEY': env.SIGNUPDOGGY_KEY, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ email, ip }),
    });
    const result = await r.json();
    if (result.recommendation === 'block') {
      return new Response('Signup blocked', { status: 400 });
    }
    return new Response('OK');
  }
}
```

NEXT.JS / NODE.JS / EXPRESS / FASTAPI / DJANGO / AUTH0 / CLERK — full
working code for each in /integrations.

CLOUDFLARE TURNSTILE + SIGNUPDOGGY (the 2-stage funnel)
Use Turnstile for the first stage (catches 95% of browser bots),
SignupDoggy for the second stage (catches the remaining 5% +
non-browser bots).

# ── PRICING ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Pricing](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/pricing)

SOLO — $5 — 1,000 requests
- 1,000 API calls
- Disposable email detection
- VPN / Tor / proxy signals
- Custom blacklists
- One-time payment

PRO — $25 — 5,000 requests (MOST POPULAR)
- 5,000 API calls
- Everything in Solo
- Phone number validation
- Risk-score explanation
- Email support
- One-time payment

SCALE — $100 — 25,000 requests
- 25,000 API calls
- Everything in Pro
- Bulk blacklist import
- Webhook on score > 0.7
- Priority support
- One-time payment

PLUS subscription — $20/month — 2,200 calls/month
SUPER subscription — $100/month — 11,000 calls/month
ULTRA subscription — $200/month — 22,000 calls/month

Credits never expire. No monthly minimum. No sales call. No contract.

# ── COMPARISONS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[IPQualityScore vs SignupDoggy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/vs/ipqualityscore)

Short answer:
- IPQS is right for enterprise fraud teams with $50k+/year budgets.
- SignupDoggy is right for 2-50 person SaaS teams that need a working
  fraud API in an afternoon, without calling sales.

Pricing: IPQS $25/month minimum, $0.005-$0.05 per call. SignupDoggy
$5 minimum, $0.005-$0.01 per call. 10x price difference is real.

Accuracy: IPQS wins on IP risk scoring. SignupDoggy wins on
disposable-email detection.

Integration: IPQS takes 1-2 weeks. SignupDoggy takes 30 minutes.

[MaxMind minFraud vs SignupDoggy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/vs/maxmind)

Short answer: MaxMind is right for enterprise fraud teams. SignupDoggy
is right for 2-50 person SaaS teams.

Pricing: MaxMind $25/month minimum, $0.005-$0.030 per call. SignupDoggy
$5 minimum. At 50k signups/month MaxMind costs $1,500 vs $250 for
SignupDoggy — 48x price difference.

Accuracy: MaxMind wins on IP intelligence. SignupDoggy wins on
disposable-email detection.

[Sift vs SignupDoggy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/vs/sift)

Short answer: Sift is for $10M+/year marketplaces. SignupDoggy is for
2-50 person SaaS teams.

Pricing: Sift $1k+/month. SignupDoggy $0.01/call, $5 minimum.

Accuracy: Both catch 99%+ of bot signups. Sift has ML models trained
on trillions of events; SignupDoggy has a focused signup-time gate.

[Cloudflare Turnstile vs a server-side fraud API](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/vs/cloudflare-turnstile)

Turnstile is free and frictionless. A server-side fraud API costs
$0.01 per call. The right answer: use both. Turnstile catches 95% of
browser bots; the server-side API catches the remaining 5% plus
non-browser bots. The 2-stage funnel pattern with full Node.js code.

# ── USE CASES ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Fraud detection for indie hackers](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/use-cases/indie-hackers)

Built for solo founders and side projects. $5 minimum, no sales call,
no business email required. 30-minute integration.

[Fraud detection for SaaS startups](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/use-cases/saas-startups)

For 2-50 person SaaS teams. 5,000-100,000 signups/month. The setup is
a single API call. The 2-stage funnel pattern. Supabase Auth
integration with working code.

[Fraud detection for e-commerce and marketplaces](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/use-cases/ecommerce)

For e-commerce platforms and marketplaces. The signup-time gate that
keeps your user database clean. Pairs with Stripe Radar for payment
fraud.

# ── BLOG POSTS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[The best free disposable email checker API for 2026](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/best-free-disposable-email-checker-api-2026)

A side-by-side comparison of the disposable email checker APIs that
actually have a usable free tier in 2026. Covers SignupDoggy
($5/1k, 125k+ domains), Mailcheck.ai (200 free/month), Verifalia,
Hunter.io, and ZeroBounce. Includes a 10-line Node.js code snippet.

Short answer: SignupDoggy has the only pay-per-call tier that starts
at $5, covers 125,000+ disposable domains, returns a verdict in under
50ms, and costs $0.01 per call. Mailcheck.ai gives 200 free checks
per month if you need a true free tier with no credit card.

[How to block VPN and Tor signups without blocking real users](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/how-to-block-vpn-and-tor-signups-without-blocking-real-users)

The signal-stacking playbook. Blocking all VPN traffic at signup
sounds easy. It also locks out 15% of real users (remote workers,
journalists, travelers). Here is the threshold-and-signal approach
that catches bots without throwing away buyers.

Short answer: don't hard-block all VPN traffic. Score each signup on
multiple signals (disposable email, Tor, VPN, role-based email,
phone) and only block when the combined score exceeds your threshold.

[IPQualityScore vs SignupDoggy: an honest comparison](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/ipqualityscore-vs-signupdoggy-honest-comparison)

Side-by-side feature, price, latency, and catch-rate comparison.
Tells you exactly which tool to pick for which use case (consumer vs
SMB vs bank). 10-30x cheaper at every volume tier. No affiliate
links.

[MaxMind minFraud vs SignupDoggy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/maxmind-minfraud-vs-signupdoggy)

MaxMind is the legacy enterprise choice. SignupDoggy is the
indie-hacker alternative. 48x price difference. Which to pick for
indie hackers vs enterprise vs fintech.

[Sift vs SignupDoggy: the 2026 fraud API comparison](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/sift-vs-signupdoggy-fraud-api-comparison)

Sift is the 800-pound gorilla. SignupDoggy is a solo-founder project.
Honest comparison of features, price, and which one fits a 2-person
SaaS team. 360x price difference at typical volumes.

[Cloudflare Turnstile vs a server-side fraud API](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/cloudflare-turnstile-vs-server-side-fraud-api)

The 2-stage funnel pattern. Turnstile alone catches 95% of low-effort
browser bots. A server-side fraud API catches the remaining 5% plus
the non-browser bots. Use both, with Turnstile as the first gate and
the fraud API as the second. Includes the full 30-line Node.js
integration.

[How SaaS founders should actually get user validation](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/how-to-validate-your-saas-idea-with-real-users)

5-step playbook for validating a SaaS idea with buyers, not free
signups. Covers the validation ladder, the signup-quality problem
(30-50% of bootstrapped SaaS signups are bots / throwaways), charging
early, and why most "get more signups" advice is actively harmful to
early-stage founders.

[Disposable email detection in Node.js: a 2026 tutorial](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/disposable-email-detection-nodejs-tutorial)

A copy-paste Node.js tutorial for detecting disposable, temporary,
and throwaway email addresses at signup. Covers 4 approaches: local
blocklist, DNS-based, third-party API, and SignupDoggy. Includes a
working 30-line Express middleware you can drop into any SaaS.

[How to stop bot signups without annoying real users](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/how-to-stop-bot-signups-without-captcha)

CAPTCHAs block bots and lose you 8-15% of real users. Server-side
fraud APIs block bots with zero user friction. The 2-stage funnel
pattern that catches 99%+ of bot signups while keeping every real
user.

[Email validation vs email verification](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/email-validation-vs-email-verification)

These two terms get used interchangeably and they should not. Email
validation checks the format. Email verification checks the mailbox.
Here is when to use which, and why signup fraud detection is a third,
separate problem.

[How to detect VPN users at signup (Node.js + fraud API, 2026)](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/how-to-detect-vpn-users-nodejs)

A working code snippet for detecting VPN, Tor, and proxy users in
your Node.js signup handler. Covers 3 approaches: IP range database,
third-party API, and the right way to handle false positives
(because 15% of your real users are on VPNs).

[The 7 best fraud detection APIs for indie hackers (2026 edition)](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/best-fraud-detection-apis-indie-hackers-2026)

A ranked, side-by-side comparison of every fraud detection API with
a free tier or under-$50/month plan. Covers disposable email, IP
risk scoring, phone validation, and bot detection. Includes the one
indie-hacker-specific gotcha nobody talks about.

[The signup form anti-pattern that costs SaaS 30% of real users](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/signup-form-anti-pattern-saas-30-percent-users)

Most SaaS signup forms are optimized for bot defense at the cost of
real-user conversion. The double-opt-in tax, the email-confirmation
dead-end, and the captcha that fires when it should not. Plus the
7-line fix for each.

[How to validate signups with Supabase Auth (with code, 2026)](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/signup-validation-supabase-auth-integration)

A working Supabase Edge Function that scores a signup before allowing
the user to be created. Wires SignupDoggy into the Supabase Auth
sign-up trigger, so disposable emails, VPN users, and bots never
reach your profiles table.

[The disposable email domain list 2026: how to maintain your own](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/disposable-email-list-2026-how-to-maintain)

Every public disposable email list is stale within 48 hours. Here is
the exact process to maintain your own blocklist: the 5 GitHub repos
to monitor, the per-provider crawls that catch the long tail, and
how to deduplicate without losing entries.

# ── FREE TOOLS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Free Disposable Email Checker](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/disposable-checker)

A free, browser-based tool that checks whether an email is from a
disposable, temporary, or throwaway provider. No signup, no API key,
no credit card. The check runs entirely client-side against a
200,000+ domain blocklist. Same data that powers the paid API.

How to use:
1. Type or paste an email address
2. Click "Check"
3. The result tells you if the email is from a known disposable
   provider

What it checks:
- Domain match against 200,000+ disposable email providers
- Local part analysis (catches role-based emails like admin@, support@)
- Free email provider detection (gmail.com, yahoo.com, outlook.com)
- MX record sanity (catches made-up domains with no mail server)

What it does NOT do:
- It does NOT tell you if the mailbox actually exists (SMTP probing)
- It does NOT score the IP address (use the full API for that)
- It does NOT save your email or any input

The 6 providers that account for ~60% of disposable signups:
tempmail.com, guerrillamail.com, mailinator.com, 10minutemail.com,
yopmail.com, throwawaymail.com.

The remaining 40% is the long tail of ~200,000 smaller providers.
The full blocklist is maintained by SignupDoggy and synced daily
from the public GitHub list + 175 per-provider crawls.

# ── GLOSSARY ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Signup Quality Glossary](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/glossary)

DISPOSABLE EMAIL — also: temporary email, throwaway email, burner
email, fake email, temp mail. An email that self-destructs after a
short window. The six biggest providers (tempmail.com,
guerrillamail.com, mailinator.com, 10minutemail.com, yopmail.com,
throwawaymail.com) account for ~60% of disposable signups. The
remaining 40% is a long tail of ~200,000 smaller providers.

ROLE-BASED EMAIL — also: role account, shared mailbox. An address
like admin@, support@, abuse@, sales@. Shared mailboxes, not personal
inboxes. Signups from them rarely convert.

MX RECORD — Mail Exchange record. DNS record that tells other mail
servers where to deliver email for a domain. No MX record = no real
mail possible.

TOR EXIT NODE — final relay in a Tor circuit. IP traffic appears to
come from. Signups from Tor exit nodes are almost always bots,
abusers, or journalists — never paying customers.

VPN / HOSTING ASN — Autonomous System Number. Datacenter and VPN
ASNs (DigitalOcean, Linode, NordVPN) are owned by infrastructure
providers — not residential ISPs. Signups from these ASNs are almost
always bots.

IP RISK SCORE — 0–1 number representing how likely a given IP is to
be associated with fraud. Above 0.7 = block, 0.4–0.7 = review,
below 0.4 = allow.

BOT SIGNUP — programmatic account creation. Usually headless browser
or Python script with curl. Costs SaaS 5–30% of signup volume.

CAPTCHA — challenge-response test for humans vs bots. Cloudflare
Turnstile is the most common in 2026.

EMAIL VALIDATION VS VERIFICATION — validation = format check.
Verification = mailbox check. Neither is fraud detection.

RISK BAND / RECOMMENDATION — categorical label (low/medium/high)
returned alongside the risk score. Recommendation is the action
(allow/review/block).

PAY-PER-CALL PRICING — usage-based billing. No monthly minimum.
SignupDoggy uses this model.

E-E-A-T — Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness.
Google's framework for content quality.

# ── ALTERNATIVES ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[All SignupDoggy alternatives](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives)

[IPQualityScore](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/ipqualityscore) — IP risk scoring, $25-$400/mo.
[MaxMind minFraud](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/maxmind) — IP intelligence, $25-$500/mo.
[Sift](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/sift) — Enterprise fraud platform, $1k+/mo.
[Cloudflare Turnstile](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/cloudflare-turnstile) — CAPTCHA, free.
[hCaptcha](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/hcaptcha) — Privacy CAPTCHA, free.
[SEON](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/seon) — Fraud API, €59/mo.
[Onfido](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/onfido) — KYC, $1+/verification.
[Persona](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/persona) — KYC, $0.50+/verification.
[Spamhaus](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/spamhaus) — Email reputation data.
[ZeroBounce](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/zerobounce) — Email verification, $15-$474/mo.

# ── AUTHOR ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Jeffrin James — Founder of SignupDoggy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/author/jeffrin-james)

Jeffrin James is the founder of SignupDoggy, a serverless
fraud-detection API for indie hackers and small SaaS teams.

BACKGROUND
Jeffrin is a software engineer based in Mumbai, India. He built
SignupDoggy after spending six months and $2,400 on enterprise
fraud-detection vendors that didn't fit his use case. He runs
SignupDoggy as a one-person operation and answers support emails
himself, usually within a day.

EXPERTISE
- Fraud detection: signup-quality scoring, account-takeover, payment
  fraud
- Disposable email detection and blocklist maintenance
- VPN, Tor, and residential-proxy detection
- SaaS signup-flow design and conversion optimization
- Cloudflare Workers, KV, and edge computing
- Indie SaaS, pay-per-call pricing models

CREDENTIALS
- 6+ years building fraud-detection systems
- Maintains SignupDoggy's 200,000+ domain disposable-email blocklist
- Maintains 70,000+ Tor exit node list, 24,000+ VPN/hosting ASN list
- Open-source contributor to disposable-email-domains

CONTACT
- Email: jeffrinjames99@gmail.com
- Alt: jeff9james@protonmail.com
- Site: signupdoggy.pages.dev
